title: From The Temporary To The Eternal Lessons From The Life Of The Apostle Paul
scriptureForWeek: Make sure you follow along with the days of the week as we will be looking at some of the clearest and most compelling verses about sexuality in the scriptures.
weekTheme: Boys lust; Men love God will upgrade our sexuality God and sex. These 2 words feel like some of the weirdest words in the English language to be in the same sentence. Yet the Bible teaches that these actually go together all the time. Believe it or not, God is not the great spoilsport of sex; God is the inventor of sex. Far from being seen as dirty or naughty, sex was actually God's idea. He created it as a gift for married couples to enjoy each other’s love, create kids, and remember the great story of God's love. But sex has been distorted and abused in the world today. It’s been taken out of its context and misused so it’s become a threat and not a gift to so many in the world. Paul lived at a time when sex was used for power, exploitation, and entertainment. The Roman Empire had lots of exploitive and abusive practices around sex. And the Romans thought it was normal. For them, sex wasn’t part of some great design. It was just something that you did with your body for pleasure, power, or procreation. Sex was cheap, dirty, and driven by lust. Paul saw this everywhere, and wanted Christians living in the Roman Empire to view sex differently. He wanted them to know that God had a plan for sex that was better than what the Romans were doing with it. If you could sit down with the Apostle Paul and ask him about what's important in our relationship with Jesus, you may be surprised at how

days.title: Power And Potency
days.scripture: HEBREWS 13:4 Give honor to marriage, and remain faithful to one another in marriage. God will surely judge people who are immoral and those who commit adultery.
days.reflection: A group of teenage guys in Oregon were playing with fireworks one day when one of them carelessly threw one down into a ravine below them. Almost immediately smoke began to rise. The boy who threw the fireworks was just 15 years old, and he didn’t know it at the time but he had just started the Eagle Creek Canyon fire, which would end up taking firefighters months to put out and burn an area as large as Washington DC. People’s homes were burned to the ground, and over 40 million dollars worth of damage was done. A woman who saw him throw the fireworks said to him, “Do you realize you just started a forest fire?” The answer at the time was no. The boy was caught by police and sentenced to 5 years probation and almost 2000 hours of community service. He was then ordered to pay 36 million dollars in restitution for the damage he had done. That guy had no idea at the time the damage that fire could do. Fire is a powerful force. Fire inside a fireplace is amazing. It can warm a whole house, create a killer vibe, and let you cook s'mores over it. But if the fire gets out of the fireplace, it can burn the whole house down. Sex is kind of like fire. It’s a potent force in the world. God designed sex to be used in the context of marriage when a couple has made a whole life commitment to one another before God. In that setting, sex is an amazing gift. It can warm a marriage, create kids, and be a lot of fun. But if sex gets outside of the boundaries God sets for it (outside the fireplace), it can burn a whole life down. That’s why the passage today gives such intense warnings about sex happening in the wrong place. God isn’t trying to be harsh when it comes to sex; he is trying to put a boundary around it to make sure we don’t get burned by it. He warns us to make sure we keep it in the right place, inside of a marriage. Of course, God can forgive our sexual sins, and Jesus will have mercy on us. Jesus was so kind to people who had sinned sexually in the gospel, but he is trying to save us from the heartache, guilt, and shame that often go along with it. It's totally natural for guys your age to think about sex and sexuality. It’s an important part of life. But you need to think about it like a godly man, one who understands the gift of it as well as the damage it can do. So you need to be wise about sex and not just carried along by your hormones. Something as small as sex during the teenage years can have eternal consequences. You have to learn to think about that in the midst of all the chaos and confusion around sexuality that’s happening around you. Learn to think about sex the way God intends and you will be wiser than you can comprehend. And who doesn’t want that?
questions: If sex is such a powerful force, why do you think our culture talks about it so casually?
If God invented sex, why do you think people think he hates it?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share the best advice they got about sex when they were growing up. How can they see that it was helpful now that they are a man?

thoughts.verse: Read through slowly. My son, obey your father’s commands, and don’t neglect your mother’s instruction. Keep their words always in your heart. Tie them around your neck. When you walk, their counsel will lead you. When you sleep, they will protect you. When you wake up, they will advise you. For their command is a lamp and their instruction a light; their corrective discipline is the way to life. It will keep you from the immoral woman, from the smooth tongue of a promiscuous woman. Don’t lust for her beauty. Don’t let her coy glances seduce you. For a prostitute will bring you to poverty, but sleeping with another man’s wife will cost you your life. Can a man scoop a flame into his lap and not have his clothes catch on fire? Can he walk on hot coals and not blister his feet? So it is with the man who sleeps with another man’s wife. He who embraces her will not go unpunished. Proverbs 6:20-29 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you a vision of godly sexuality and to help you see through the lies and distortion around sex in our modern world.

days.scripture: 1 THESSALONIANS 4:3-8 God’s will is for you to be holy, so stay away from all sexual sin. Then each of you will control his own body and live in holiness and honor—not in lustful passion like the pagans who do not know God and his ways. Never harm or cheat a fellow believer in this matter by violating his wife, for the Lord avenges all such sins, as we have solemnly warned you before. God has called us to live holy lives, not impure lives. Therefore, anyone who refuses to live by these rules is not disobeying human teaching but is rejecting God, who gives his Holy Spirit to you.
days.reflection: Learning new things is hard. It can be really challenging to learn a new subject at school like algebra or history. It can be hard to learn a new sport or a new language as well. You can feel awkward like you aren’t getting it and like you keep making mistakes. But learning new things as you grow is part of what it means to grow from being a boy into becoming a man. Believe it or not, the Bible tells us that we have to think about sex like that. We have to learn to think about it differently than the world does and learn to control ourselves in a godly way. Paul wrote the words in today’s readings to a new church in a city called Thessalonica, (it’s still a real place that you can visit today.) The people who met Jesus there and became his disciples were from all different backgrounds with all different views about sex. Some of them had had a lot of sexual experience, and most of them used to think about sex like everyone else did in the Roman Empire, which taught that sex was just a bodily function like eating or sleeping. But now that they were followers of Jesus, they had to learn how to live in light of their relationships with Jesus and his eternal kingdom. This meant that a lot of what they used to think and did had to change. They had to learn how to see sex as a gift to be stewarded, not an appetite to be fulfilled. You are going to have to learn these lessons too. Our world today thinks that the purpose of life is really about partying and pleasure. Getting as much money as you can, having as much sex as you can, and having as much fun as you can. But Paul knew that this didn’t really fill a man’s heart. We were born for something more satisfying and meaningful than just having fun. We were born to be leaders on a mission to restore the whole world. So what did Paul tell the Thessalonians they needed to learn about sex, and what would he say to us? God wants you to be holy and honorable. So many men in the world today have a perverted sexuality and are filled with shame because of their secret addictions. God wants you to be holy and honorable. He wants you to be able to hold your head high as a disciple of Jesus. This isn’t about just “being good” or “trying not to sin.” It’s about being a noble and godly man. God wants you to learn to control yourself. God doesn’t want you to be a slave to sex or controlled by physical desire. He wants you to direct your body in the right direction. He wants you to be in charge of your desires, not addicted to them. He wants to train you to use your body in the right way. God wants you to treat women with respect. So many men today treat women like a piece of property, or they objectify women as sexual objects. Paul taught that women should be treated with respect and not taken advantage of by men. You should be a man that women feel safe and respected around. God is serious about this. The world thinks it can treat sexuality however it wants without any kind of consequences. But God is serious about his standards. His teachings aren’t just human opinions that can be changed as the world wants them to. As you grow up, you are going to make mistakes, and you are going to be surrounded by crazy amounts of temptation. You are going to have to learn to think and act in a way that honors God, women, and yourself. But God will walk with you and help you on this journey. He will show you grace when you struggle and give you power to overcome temptation. Learning about sex from a godly perspective is one of the biggest advantages you will learn on The Primal Path. At times, you may feel a bit weird compared to your friends or even like you are missing out. But as time goes on, you will see the life, joy, and wisdom in following God's way. He invented sex after all and can give you the best advice ever. And besides, learning about sex is better than learning a language or algebra ☺
questions: Where do most guys your age learn their views about sex? Do you see this helping or hindering them?
What are some practical things you can do now to make girls feel respected and safe?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they learned to think about sex in a godly way and not as the world does. What things helped shape their thinking on this?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. So put to death the sinful, earthly things lurking within you. Have nothing to do with sexual immorality, impurity, lust, and evil desires. Don’t be greedy, for a greedy person is an idolater, worshiping the things of this world. Because of these sins, the anger of God is coming. You used to do these things when your life was still part of this world. But now is the time to get rid of anger, rage, malicious behavior, slander, and dirty language. Don’t lie to each other, for you have stripped off your old sinful nature and all its wicked deeds. Colossians 3:5-9 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you his vision for sex. Ask him to free you from any lies you might be believing based on what the world says.

days.title: An Embodied Faith
days.scripture: 1 CORINTHIANS 6:12-20 You say, “I am allowed to do anything”—but not everything is good for you. And even though “I am allowed to do anything,” I must not become a slave to anything. You say, “Food was made for the stomach, and the stomach for food.” (This is true, though someday God will do away with both of them.) But you can’t say that our bodies were made for sexual immorality. They were made for the Lord, and the Lord cares about our bodies. And God will raise us from the dead by his power, just as he raised our Lord from the dead. Don’t you realize that your bodies are actually parts of Christ? Should a man take his body, which is part of Christ, and join it to a prostitute? Never! And don’t you realize that if a man joins himself to a prostitute, he becomes one body with her? For the Scriptures say, “The two are united into one.” But the person who is joined to the Lord is one spirit with him. Run from sexual sin! No other sin so clearly affects the body as this one does. For sexual immorality is a sin against your own body. Don’t you realize that your body is the temple of the Holy Spirit, who lives in you and was given to you by God? You do not belong to yourself, for God bought you with a high price. So you must honor God with your body.
days.reflection: Do you know what dualism is? It’s a weird word that almost no one talks about, but when Paul was planting churches in the Roman Empire, it was how most people thought about life. Dualism taught that the spirit was good and pure but that physical matter was evil or second-rate. Dualists believed that what you did with your body didn’t really matter, as long as your spirit was good. This is totally different than what Christians believe. We believe that God made the world and that creation is a gift of God. We believe that God cares about all of life and that what we do with our bodies really affects our faith and discipleship. Some Christians have a bad belief that says that when we die we will just become a disembodied spirit floating around forever in a spiritual realm called heaven. But this is wrong. We will be given a new body, and we will live in a new heaven and earth. Our faith is an embodied faith. The Corinthian church was a church that Paul started in the city of Corinth. Corinth was a really wild city. It was kind of like the Las Vegas of its day. It was full of prostitution, sex, and partying. The people in the church used to do all the crazy things other people did in the city, but when they met Jesus, they knew they needed to start living like disciples and following God's plan for their lives. But there was one area they were slow learners and it was around sex. They had a really hard time getting rid of their old thinking. The Corinthians thought they could have sex with prostitutes and each other and that God didn’t care as long as they had good hearts. But Paul had to step in and correct their faulty thinking. Paul wanted them to know the following. What we do with our bodies matters. But you can’t say that our bodies were made for sexual immorality. They were made for the Lord, and the Lord cares about our bodies. And God will raise us from the dead by his power, just as he raised our Lord from the dead. Our bodies are temples where God dwells. Don’t you realize that your bodies are actually parts of Christ? Should a man take his body, which is part of Christ, and join it to a prostitute? Never! And don’t you realize that if a man joins himself to a prostitute, he becomes one body with her? When you gave your life to Jesus, you gave him your body too, not just your heart. You do not belong to yourself, for God bought you with a high price. So you must honor God with your body. As you grow into a godly man, you are going to learn that what you do with your body affects your faith and your heart. You are a whole person, not just a meat suit or a brain on a stick. Sex is not just an appetite like eating, or drinking. It’s an expression of who you love and what you value. Our culture wants you to think that your bodily desires should drive your life. But men know that their faith should control their desires. What you do with your body matters. How you use it will either draw you closer to God or draw you further away from him. Don’t be a dualist. Don’t be a Corinthian. You are a temple, not a brothel. Learn to live like that and you will find a deeper joy than you can ever comprehend, even as a young guy your age.
questions: Have you ever thought of your body as a temple that God dwells in? How does that make you feel?
What are some ways you see guys your age having distorted thinking about sex? Where do you think those ideas are coming from?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they see the role their body plays in their faith. How have they learned to care about their body as a part of their discipleship?

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Do not let sin control the way you live; do not give in to sinful desires. Do not let any part of your body become an instrument of evil to serve sin. Instead, give yourselves completely to God, for you were dead, but now you have new life. So use your whole body as an instrument to do what is right for the glory of God. Romans 6:12-13 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to offer yourself to God. Ask him for forgiveness for any ways you may have used your body in the wrong way, and ask him for help to get rid of any dualistic thinking you may have.

days.title: What’S Appropriate
days.scripture: EPHESIANS 5:3-14 Let there be no sexual immorality, impurity, or greed among you. Such sins have no place among God’s people. Obscene stories, foolish talk, and coarse jokes—these are not for you. Instead, let there be thankfulness to God. You can be sure that no immoral, impure, or greedy person will inherit the Kingdom of Christ and of God. For a greedy person is an idolater, worshiping the things of this world. Don’t be fooled by those who try to excuse these sins, for the anger of God will fall on all who disobey him. Don’t participate in the things these people do. For once you were full of darkness, but now you have light from the Lord. So live as people of light! For this light within you produces only what is good and right and true. Carefully determine what pleases the Lord. Take no part in the worthless deeds of evil and darkness; instead, expose them. It is shameful even to talk about the things that ungodly people do in secret. But their evil intentions will be exposed when the light shines on them, for the light makes everything visible. This is why it is said, “Awake, O sleeper, rise up from the dead, and Christ will give you light.”
days.reflection: Have you ever been hanging out with your friends when someone starts saying dumb stuff? They start saying things that are crude and rude. Things that are inappropriate. Learning how to act in an appropriate way is a really important part of growing up as a man. Boys often don’t know how to act appropriately in different situations. Sometimes they say silly things. Sometimes they are rude to people or just don’t know how to act. They can hurt people’s feelings and at times make people lose respect for them. When it comes to thinking and talking about sex, it’s important that you learn how to act appropriately as a Christian. In today's reading, Paul was writing to the church in Ephesus. Ephesus was another city that was filled with godless sexuality. The city was dominated by the goddess Artemis, who was a woman with breasts all over the front of her. She had a huge temple at the center of the city, and it was filled with temple prostitutes. These prostitutes offered their bodies to the goddess and asked her to fill their bodies with her spirit. Then men would have sex with the prostitutes and hope that their spirits would connect with the spirit of the goddess inside the prostitute. Kind of wild hey? When the Ephesians became followers of Jesus, they had to stop visiting Artemis’ temple and stop having sex with the prostitutes. Why? They served a different God, and they gave themselves to Jesus. It wasn’t appropriate to do that anymore. Paul was urging the Ephesian Christians to learn to act appropriately based on who they now were. That’s why he said: Let there be no sexual immorality, impurity, or greed among you. Such sins have no place among God’s people. Obscene stories, foolish talk, and coarse jokes—these are not for you. These are not for you. This is what Paul wants Christians to know. You may not live in a world where people have sex with temple prostitutes to worship a false God, but you do live in a world where there are tons of things that are not appropriate for men who follow Jesus. Sleeping around, looking at porn, and making dirty jokes are what the world does - this is not what godly men do. So we need to learn to put these things behind us and live like God wants us to. Full of power and joy and love. Some people may tell us that it’s not a big deal, but they are wrong. Paul wrote some serious warnings to people like that. He said: Don’t be fooled by those who try to excuse these sins, for the anger of God will fall on all who disobey him. Don’t participate in the things these people do. Paul isn’t trying to make you look like a jerk in front of your friends. He is wanting to make sure you don’t act like a jerk in front of Jesus. He wants to teach you how to get rid of your shame and live with confidence. He wants you to learn how to act appropriately in the kingdom of God. After all, that’s the place that matters most.
questions: Do you think there are any sorts of idols that people worship in our world today? Not like in biblical times, but things they put too much importance on or allow to take the place of God? What would a couple of them be?
In what ways do you see guys your age acting inappropriately when it comes to sex? What are some ways you can act differently without looking like a self-righteous jerk?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share some ways they have learned to resist temptation and not go along with the crowd without seeming stupid.

thoughts.verse: Read through these verses slowly. Don’t you realize that those who do wrong will not inherit the Kingdom of God? Don’t fool yourselves. Those who indulge in sexual sin, or who worship idols, or commit adultery, or are male prostitutes, or practice homosexuality, or are thieves, or greedy people, or drunkards, or are abusive, or cheat people—none of these will inherit the Kingdom of God. Some of you were once like that. But you were cleansed; you were made holy; you were made right with God by calling on the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God. 1 Corinthians 6:9-11 Read through these verses slowly a second time. Is there something that stands out to you in it? Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean? Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to teach you how to act appropriately as a child of God. Ask him for wisdom on how to be different from the world without being unnecessarily weird.

days.title: Way Better Worship
days.scripture: ROMANS 12:1-2 And so, dear brothers and sisters, I plead with you to give your bodies to God because of all he has done for you. Let them be a living and holy sacrifice—the kind he will find acceptable. This is truly the way to worship him. Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect.
days.reflection: Have you ever given in to peer pressure? All of us have at some point in our lives. It’s amazing how other people can get us to do things we don’t want and act in ways we know are wrong just to fit in. The early Christians lived in a world that kept trying to pressure them to give up their beliefs and fit in with what the Roman Empire wanted them to believe. But Paul wanted the disciples in Rome to give themselves to God instead as an act of worship. He wanted them to have their thinking and behavior transformed by God's truth and will for their lives. It’s important to note that for Paul, worship isn’t just singing some songs at church or an emotional feeling at a youth event. Worship involves your body. It’s about the whole of your life. Worship isn’t about singing with your lips and then sinning with your body; it’s about an integrated life with God in all you do. That’s worship at another level. And note something very important here: worship isn’t based on guilt or fear. Worship is based on God's mercy!! It's based on gratitude for the kindness he has shown us in sending Jesus to reveal God's love and take away our sins. Worship is based on gratitude, not guilt. You live in a world that wants to pressure you to think and act the way it does about sex. The world wants you to see sex as nothing but a bit of fun, without any consequences, that you can use however you want. It wants you to think porn is harmless, sex is no big deal, and nothing you do with sex is really wrong if you are two consenting adults. But this kind of thinking is built on thin lies that we all know aren’t true. God made sex with a purpose, to happen in the context of marriage, to be used as a gift to build our spouse up in love. It’s not just a kind of recreational pleasure two consenting adults do when they have the desire. Sex can be holy, and sex can be sinful. Sex can lead to blessing, or sex can bring brokenness. So learn to offer your body to God in the right way and live a life of wholeness and holiness before him. This week, you have looked at how God's vision of sex lifts our vision of it from temporary pleasure into a force that can shape our lives for eternity. You have seen how what you do with your body deeply affects your heart. You have seen that giving in to sexual temptation can lead to shame and regret that isn’t worth the momentary pleasure. You have seen pagan sexuality isn’t appropriate for people who follow Jesus, because they are called to something higher and more holy. And you have seen how you are going to have to learn to control yourself in an honorable way so that you don’t become a slave to the whims and desires of your body. But learning about this and living this out are two very different things. Sex is one of the most powerful forces in a man's life, and it can be a real struggle to learn to honor God in this area of your life. Don’t hate yourself if you mess up, and don’t hide your sin out of shame. Bring it into the light, confess it before God, and keep moving forward in your life. God is patient with us and rich in mercy. And he will give us the power we need to resist temptation and walk in the light. It turns out that sex and God do belong in the same sentence because both things belong in our lives. We just need to learn how they belong in a godly way. Offer your body to God in worship and not to lust in the heat of passion, and you will slowly become the man you are called to be. A godly man who, by the grace of God, can hold his head high.
questions: Can you name a time when you gave in to peer pressure? What did you do, and who put the pressure on you to do it?
What are some areas that you think God may want to transform from worldly thinking to godly thinking in the life of someone your age?

Ask the person you are doing this study with to share how they learned to deal with peer pressure as a man. How did they get the courage to resist and be true to their convictions? Ask them for any advice they may have for you.

thoughts.verse: Run from anything that stimulates youthful lusts. Instead, pursue righteous living, faithfulness, love, and peace. Enjoy the companionship of those who call on the Lord with pure hearts.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to transform your thinking and to give you an understanding of what his will is for you. Thank him for his leadership and direction in your life.

extras: WEEK 3 MANSCHOOL DATE NIGHT MAN SCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: DATE NIGHT One of the hardest things for young guys today is to really understand what it is that women value and how to treat them with respect. Women are so often sexualized and treated with contempt in our society. So have your son take his mom out for a meal, and talk to her about how she likes to be treated. What do men need to know about women? How do women view men in the modern world, and what does he need to get right? This can be a low-key dinner, and you obviously need to ask your wife if she is cool having this kind of conversation. Again, it doesn’t have to be super heavy, just establishing bridges of communication on these important but sensitive topics. OPTION 2. WATCH FIREPROOF This is an older film and a touch on the cheesy side, but it does a decent job of explaining what real love looks like and how a man needs to fight for his marriage. A more compelling vision of marriage than the cheap vision of sex and self-fulfillment our world pushes on young guys today. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=84q0SXW781c OPTION 3. DINNER AND DISCUSSION Take your son out to one of his favorite restaurants or for desserts and talk through what sex and faith look like in the modern world. Now this could obviously be a bit awkward, so keep it cool and don’t try and push
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
